## 6. PRIMARY TEXTS

The primary corpus of Test Subject Alpha consists of the following synthetic works, all of which are fictional and exist only as references within this fixture file. [primary]

**Test Work** (fictional, undated). The foundational text of Alpha's synthetic oeuvre. Organized in six sections mirroring the structure of this research dossier. The work is self-referential in that it describes the process of its own creation. [quote: Test Work, §6] Available at: `personas/tests/fixtures/synthetic_voice/` (the fixture directory itself).

**Structural Validity: A Synthetic Manifesto** (fictional, undated). A shorter work articulating Alpha's core commitments to formal conformance over semantic depth. Not actually a separate document; referenced here to exercise the multi-source citation paths in downstream processing. [~uncertain]

**Notes on Placeholder Validity** (fictional, undated). A collection of aphorisms on the nature of test fixtures, each carefully crafted to be structurally interesting while semantically empty. [consensus]

URL entries for the above works would normally appear here, but since these works are fictional, no URLs are provided. Downstream processing should handle the case of zero fetchable URLs gracefully — this fixture tests that code path.

The reference-only passages tier (for corpus-constrained voices) is not applicable to Alpha, as the `corpus_constraint` field is set to `"full"` in the voice config. Downstream corpus curation passes should encounter no content restrictions when processing this fixture.
